label: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 8

extraction.one_line_summary: KUSS Sepp attacked 3km from final climb summit, gained enough time to take race lead while ROGLIC won stage sprint from reduced group
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma deployed VALTER and VINGEGAARD pacing to isolate MARTINEZ, preventing EVENEPOEL from attacking with three-rider numerical advantage retained.
KUSS attacked for race lead not stage win, demonstrating team hierarchy where GC ambition splits from stage hunting responsibilities.
EVENEPOEL led out finale despite being outnumbered three-to-three, revealing reluctance to let UAE/Jumbo control closing kilometers even when tactically disadvantaged.

rider_signals.MARTINEZ Lenny: negative: Lost 1:09 to leaders 3km from summit, showing insufficient climbing form when pace lifted beyond threshold on steep gradients
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: Attacked for time gaps rather than stage victory, demonstrating confidence in sustained climbing power over explosive finish speed
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: Coped extraordinarily well with steep percentages but led out sprint from disadvantaged position, suggesting frustration with tactical isolation
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: Reserved enough after hard climb to outsprint AYUSO and EVENEPOEL, indicating superior recovery within reduced gruppo
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: positive: Second place from steep finish despite crash earlier, UAE's three-rider presence signals genuine GC depth beyond ALMEIDA
